records.display_name: May 22 Timetable Change

payload.description: LIRR schedule adjustments beginning May 22, 2023 through September 5, 2023 to support construction activities.

records.display_name: Cherry Valley Bridge Waterproofing

payload.description: Hempstead Branch between Floral Park and Hempstead taken out of service for approximately 24 hours while Cherry Valley Bridge in Garden City is waterproofed; bus service replaces train service in both directions.

payload.description: Long Island Committee meeting to consider the May timetable change and track work programs. Board Action: LI COMM 4/24/23.

records.display_name: Long Beach and Far Rockaway Rail Replacement Weekends

payload.description: Weekend rail replacement and signal maintenance on Long Beach and Far Rockaway Branches; bus service replaces train service between Valley Stream and Far Rockaway for 48-hour periods each weekend.

records.display_name: LIRR Main Line Expansion (Third Track)

description: Added a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line reducing train congestion and enabling bi-directional service during peak hours. Included elimination of eight street level grade crossings, improvements to rail bridges, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.
Continuing project supported during the May 2023 timetable change.
Completion of the Main Line Expansion enabling never-before-seen service flexibility, with a doubling of off-peak service on the Ronkonkoma branch
LIRR Main Line Expansion including Willis Avenue Grade Crossing Elimination, Carle Place Station, Merillon Avenue Station

Project will add a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line between Floral Park and Hicksville to reduce train congestion and enable bi-directional service during peak hours. Includes elimination of eight street level grade crossings, improvements to rail bridges, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.
Adds 10-mile third track on the LIRR Main Line between Floral Park and Hicksville; includes elimination of 8 grade crossings, bridge improvements, 5 station reconstructions, and new parking garages

Adds a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line between Floral Park and Hicksville to reduce train congestion and enable bi-directional service during peak hours. Includes elimination of eight grade crossings, rail bridge improvements, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.

payload.description: Added a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line reducing train congestion and enabling bi-directional service during peak hours. Included elimination of eight street level grade crossings, improvements to rail bridges, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.

records.display_name: Cherry Valley Bridge Waterproofing

payload.description: On Saturday, May 13 both main tracks on the Hempstead Branch between Floral Park and Hempstead will be taken out of service for approximately 24 hours while the Cherry Valley Bridge in Garden City is waterproofed.

location_normalized.raw_text: Cherry Valley Bridge, Garden City, Hempstead Branch between Floral Park and Hempstead

payload.project_name: Cherry Valley Bridge Waterproofing on the Hempstead Branch

records.display_name: Rail Replacement - Long Beach and Far Rockaway Branches

payload.description: On weekends April 29-30, May 6-7, and May 20-21, one of two main tracks on the Long Beach Branch and both main tracks on the Far Rockaway Branch will be out of service for 48 hours while rail is replaced and signal maintenance performed.
payload.project_name: Rail Replacement and Maintenance on the Long Beach and Far Rockaway Branches

records.display_name: East Side Access

description: Project bringing LIRR into Grand Central Terminal. Committed $10.2 billion as of December 2021. Current project budget $11.133 billion plus rolling stock reserve of $463 million. Public revenue service date forecast for December 2022.
The Committee will be briefed on the status of the East Side Access Support Projects.
East Side Access (ESA) project to bring LIRR service to Grand Central Madison, increasing peak service by 40% and off-peak service by 65%
Project affecting LIRR service including Atlantic Terminal to Hempstead/Far Rockaway one-seat ride changes
Continuing project supported during the May 2023 timetable change.

Design and construction of final fit-out elements within the new Grand Central Madison terminal, including LED lighting, ceiling modifications, architectural enhancements, retail spaces, digital advertising, and wayfinding signage
Now known as Grand Central Madison (GCM). Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal.
Program within the 2010-2014 Capital Program with $3,192.9 million budget. Remaining rolling stock reserves of $463 million for future M9A growth fleet for LIRR.
Systems installation and integration contract including fire suppression system. Modification to replace fire standpipe valves in the tunnels and retest the fire suppression system.
Modification to the systems installation and integration contract (CS179) for East Side Access to replace fire standpipe valves throughout the tunnels and retest the fire suppression system.

Contract modification for furnishing and installing approximately 9,500 feet of 500 MCM stray current drainage cable for C04 and C05 Traction Power Substations for the East Side Access Project. Awarded to E-J Electric Installation Co. for $1,510,000.

Retroactive modification to Tutor Perini Corporation contract for revisions to Building Management, Fire Command Center, and fire detection/suppression systems. $2,363,601. Includes joint MNR-LIRR Fire Command Center at GCT.
Extension of the Owner Controlled Insurance Program (OCIP) for the East Side Access Project through March 31, 2023. Total additional funding requested: $48.8 million.
Owner Controlled Insurance Program extension for the East Side Access Project through March 31, 2023. Revenue service anticipated by December 31, 2022.
LIRR East Side Access to Grand Central Terminal

Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal. Involves building out new 11.5-mile underground tunnels with a new station and concourse below the existing Grand Central Terminal. Consists of roughly 50 separate third-party contracts of which eight are active along with six in-house force account construction contracts.
Project bringing LIRR service to Grand Central Terminal, including Harold Structures and Systems Facilities packages
Major construction completed on the East Side Access project
Terminal and right-of-way operating and maintenance plans for the East Side Access 'Mega' project.

Modification No. 51 to the AECOM program management consultant contract for the East Side Access project, seeking additional funding for remaining 14 months of Current Option and inclusion of a Close-Out Option.
Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal via 11.5 mile underground tunnel with new 8-track terminal and concourse below Grand Central Terminal
Ramp-up costs for East Side Access readiness efforts included in LIRR budget
C&D safety report for East Side Access project covering December 8, 2021 through January 4, 2022
C&D Delivery Monthly Report for East Side Access project
Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal. 11.5 mile underground tunnel with new station and concourse below existing Grand Central Terminal.
Complete ESA PTC system testing and commence PTC operations in East Side Access territory at Grand Central
project_name: East Side Access Passenger Experience Enhancement & Finish Detailing
